findingDetails.title: compose.sh — central file in spec and research — does not exist
findingDetails.evidence: spec.md:81 describes compose.sh writing to disk; research.md:58 recommends extending compose.sh; glob for compose.sh returns 0 matches; grep for compose.sh in scripts returns 0 matches; actual renderer is inline-gate-renderer.ts/.sh
findingDetails.recommendation: Update spec.md and research.md to reflect actual manifest-based architecture or note that codebase has been refactored
findingDetails.scopeProof: Glob compose* across skill root = 0 matches; grep compose\.sh in scripts = 0 matches; inline-gate-renderer.sh exists at scripts/templates/inline-gate-renderer.sh:1-14

findingDetails.title: CORE + ADDENDUM directories do not exist — spec describes non-existent architecture
findingDetails.evidence: spec.md:56 describes CORE+ADDENDUM v2.2; spec.md:115-116 lists core/ and addendum/ as investigation targets; templates/ directory listing shows only changelog, examples, manifest, README, scratch, stress_test — no core/ or addendum/
findingDetails.recommendation: Update specification to document manifest-based architecture with templates/manifest/ as source of truth
findingDetails.scopeProof: Directory listing of templates/ confirms no core/ or addendum/ subdirectories. Grep for templates/core and templates/addendum in scripts/*.sh returns 0 matches.

findingDetails.title: level_1/ through level_3+/ output directories do not exist
findingDetails.evidence: spec.md:93 asks if level dirs can be removed; research.md:23 reports 25 files/4087 LOC in level dirs; templates/ directory listing shows no level directories; spec-kit-docs.json defines levels via JSON presets, not disk directories
findingDetails.recommendation: Update specification to accurately describe manifest-based architecture where levels are JSON presets
findingDetails.scopeProof: Directory listing of templates/ confirms no level_N/ directories. No code path references templates/level_N/ — template resolution uses templates/manifest/.

findingDetails.title: wrap-all-templates.{ts,sh} does not exist
findingDetails.evidence: spec.md:118 lists wrap-all-templates as investigation target; research.md:150 consumer migration map references it; glob for wrap-all-templates* returns 0 matches
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove references to wrap-all-templates from spec and research; document actual ANCHOR handling via template-structure.js and inline-gate-renderer.ts
findingDetails.scopeProof: Glob wrap-all-templates* across skill root = 0 matches. ANCHOR handling is in template-structure.js (parseAnchoredSections) and inline-gate-renderer.ts.

findingDetails.title: create.sh header comment describes outdated CORE + ADDENDUM architecture
findingDetails.evidence: Header comments at lines 7-10 describe CORE+ADDENDUM with level_N/ directories and LOC counts; actual code at lines 1502-1534 uses manifest-based copy_templates_batch
findingDetails.recommendation: Update header comment and --help output to reflect manifest-based architecture
findingDetails.scopeProof: Lines 1502-1534 confirm actual implementation uses copy_templates_batch with manifest resolution. Only stale references are header comment and --help text.

findingDetails.title: get_level_templates_dir maps to directories that no longer exist
findingDetails.evidence: get_level_templates_dir maps levels to level_1/level_2/level_3/level_3+/ directories that don't exist; _normalize_template_level at lines 185-197 maps old directory paths
findingDetails.recommendation: Deprecate or remove get_level_templates_dir; add comment about legacy normalization
findingDetails.scopeProof: Grep for get_level_templates_dir usage shows no active callers. Actual resolution uses _manifest_template_path and resolve_level_contract.

noveltyJustification: First iteration; all 6 findings are novel against empty prior state. P1 findings (4) carry weight 5 each; P2 findings (2) carry weight 1. Weighted new: 4×5 + 2×1 = 22. Weighted total: 22. Ratio: 22/22 = 1.0. Four P1 findings establish fundamental architecture discrepancy between spec/research and actual codebase.
edgeCases: Manifest architecture may be the result of the consolidation — the code may be post-consolidation while the investigation ran pre-consolidation
Deep-research may have targeted a stale code snapshot or branch
Ambiguity: Was PARTIAL recommendation already implemented?
template-utils.sh get_level_templates_dir may have external callers
ruledOut: P0 security findings: no exploitable issues found
Performance regression in create.sh: manifest path appears equivalent
Validator breakage from missing level dirs: template-structure.js reads from manifest path
integrationEvidence: spec-kit-docs.json — single source of truth for level contracts
level-contract-resolver.ts — TS resolver for shell callers

findingDetails.title: copy_templates_batch silently returns success on renderer failure
findingDetails.evidence: if ! "$renderer" ...; then return $? — Bash ! operator flips exit code to 0 on failure. create.sh:1527 uses $(...) || pattern that never triggers because function returns 0.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace if ! cmd; then return $? with: local rc=0; if ! cmd; then rc=$?; fi; return $rc — or use Pipestatus array.
findingDetails.scopeProof: copy_templates_batch at template-utils.sh:113-159; called from create.sh:1527 inside $(...) || guard

findingDetails.title: create_graph_metadata_file JSON injection via unescaped user input
findingDetails.evidence: ${summary_text//\"/\\\"} only escapes double-quotes. Backslashes and newlines in user FEATURE_DESCRIPTION (line 312) produce invalid JSON.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use jq or a proper JSON-escaping function. At minimum, add backslash and newline escaping to the parameter expansion.
findingDetails.scopeProof: create_graph_metadata_file at create.sh:385-458; summary_text from FEATURE_DESCRIPTION (line 312-316)

findingDetails.title: getManifestPath in template-structure.js lacks path fallback
findingDetails.evidence: getManifestPath hardcodes single path; level-contract-resolver.ts:78-86 uses two-path fallback (DEFAULT + DIST). CLI callers may fail from dist context.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add fallback resolution matching level-contract-resolver.ts resolveManifestPath pattern.
findingDetails.scopeProof: getManifestPath at template-structure.js:128-130; called by loadLevelContract (line 134) and resolveTemplatePath (line 317)

findingDetails.title: get_level_templates_dir returns paths to non-existent directories
findingDetails.evidence: Returns base_dir/level_1 etc. — all nonexistent in manifest-based architecture. No active callers found. Dead-code risk.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add deprecation comment or remove function if confirmed unused. Consider mapping to manifest-based equivalents.
findingDetails.scopeProof: get_level_templates_dir at template-utils.sh:37-47; verified no callers in reviewed code paths

findingDetails.title: Misleading SKILL_ROOT variable in inline-gate-renderer.sh
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL_ROOT resolves to scripts/ (one .. from scripts/templates/), not skill root. Works because scripts/node_modules/tsx EXISTS (verified). Variable name is semantically wrong.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename to SCRIPTS_DIR or fix path to use ../.. for actual skill root.
findingDetails.scopeProof: inline-gate-renderer.sh:9-10; verified filesystem: scripts/node_modules/tsx EXISTS, skill-root-level node_modules does NOT

noveltyJustification: All 5 findings are new — no overlap with iteration 001 (which focused on file-existence architecture mismatches). Dimension is orthogonal (code-correctness vs implementation-spec-alignment).

traceabilityChecks.determinism: CONFIRMED — inline-gate-renderer.ts renderInlineGates is pure function
traceabilityChecks.strictMode: VERIFIED — set -euo pipefail with proper if/||/&& guards

edgeCases: if ! cmd; then return $? semantic verified across Bash 3.2+ and 5.x
Leading comma in gate expression correctly errors
Trailing comma in gate expression correctly handled
isInFence toggle correctly skips gate parsing in fenced blocks
trailing-slash directory glob (line 351) safe with [[ -d ]] guard

integrationEvidence.inline-gate-renderer.sh->inline-gate-ren: Path resolution semantically misaligned (P2-002) but functionally correct
integrationEvidence.create.sh->template-utils.sh: Two rendering paths: subfolder uses copy_template (correct), normal uses copy_templates_batch (P1-001)
integrationEvidence.level-contract-resolver.ts->spec-kit-doc: Manifest loading verified; fallback resolution more robust than template-structure.js (P1-003)

findingDetails.title: spec.md.tmpl L3/3+ missing ANCHOR wrappers for risk-matrix and user-stories
findingDetails.evidence: spec-kit-docs.json sectionGates declare risk-matrix:["3","3+"] and user-stories:["3","3+"] but spec.md.tmpl L3 block at lines 517-535 has no ANCHOR:risk-matrix or ANCHOR:user-stories wrappers. Rendered L3 output: 14 ANCHOR markers (7 pairs) without risk-matrix or user-stories.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add <!-- ANCHOR:risk-matrix --> and <!-- ANCHOR:user-stories --> wrappers in spec.md.tmpl L3/3+ blocks.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Rendered spec.md.tmpl at L3 produces 14 ANCHOR markers (7 pairs); L3+ produces 22 (11 pairs) — risk-matrix and user-stories absent in both. spec-kit-docs.json:499-506 declares them.

findingDetails.title: spec.md.tmpl L3+/L3 has misplaced /ANCHOR:questions creating nested ANCHOR structure
findingDetails.evidence: ANCHOR:questions opens at line 718, closes at line 845 — wrapping approval-workflow, compliance-checkpoints, stakeholder-matrix, and change-log ANCHOR blocks as children. parseAnchoredSections() in template-structure.js:363-397 uses first open match, making interior anchors invisible as independent sections.
findingDetails.recommendation: Move /ANCHOR:questions to close before subsequent independent sections (before the NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS section).
findingDetails.scopeProof: spec.md.tmpl:718-845; L3+ rendered output shows approval-workflow, compliance-checkpoints, stakeholder-matrix, change-log as top-level anchors in grep, but parseAnchoredSections only finds outermost questions section.

findingDetails.title: tasks.md.tmpl missing dedicated Level 3 gate; uses level:3+ which excludes Level 3
findingDetails.evidence: Gates: level:1 (line 1), level:2 (line 109), level:3+ (line 325). No level:3 gate. Rendered output is identical L1=106, L2=106, L3=106 lines. SectionGates declare architecture-tasks for L3/L3+ but L3 renders without it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add <!-- IF level:3 --> gate with architecture-tasks content, OR change level:3+ gate to level:3,3+.
findingDetails.scopeProof: tasks.md.tmpl:325 IF level:3+; no IF level:3. Rendered L3 ANCHORs: completion, cross-refs, notation, phase-1, phase-2, phase-3 — missing architecture-tasks.

findingDetails.title: implementation-summary.md.tmpl has same L2/L3+ gate gap as tasks.md.tmpl
findingDetails.evidence: Gates: level:1 (line 1), level:2 (line 138), level:3+ (line 412). No level:3 gate. Rendered line counts: L1=135, L2=135, L3=135, L3+=134. SectionGates declare architecture-summary for L3/L3+ but L3 renders without it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Same fix as tasks.md.tmpl — add level:3 gate or use level:3,3+.
findingDetails.scopeProof: impl-summary.md.tmpl:412 IF level:3+; no IF level:3. Rendered output shows no differentiation between L1, L2, L3.

findingDetails.title: spec-kit-docs.json sectionGates has redundant flat top-level duplicates of spec.md nested gates
findingDetails.evidence: Flat keys like metadata, problem, scope etc. at top-level duplicate spec.md nested sectionGates with identical values. assertSectionGates processes both, overwriting with identical arrays.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove redundant flat top-level sectionGate duplicates; keep only the document-nested form.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Lines 178-219 duplicate lines 143-177 with identical level arrays.

findingDetails.title: ANCHOR:questions closure wraps 4 independent ANCHOR blocks in L3+ spec.md.tmpl
findingDetails.evidence: /ANCHOR:questions at line 845 closes after approval-workflow, compliance-checkpoints, stakeholder-matrix, change-log. These appear as nested children.
findingDetails.recommendation: Same as P1-003-002 — move /ANCHOR:questions before subsequent independent sections.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same scope as P1-003-002.

noveltyJustification: Iteration 003 covered a new dimension (template-rendering-correctness) with 6 findings: 4 P1s (missing ANCHOR wrappers, nested anchor structure, missing level gates) and 2 P2s (duplicate structure, anchor closure consequences). All findings are novel per dimension — no overlap with prior correctness or alignment findings. Weighted: 4*5+2*1 = 22 new, 0 refinement = 22/22 = 1.0 after capping at 0.694 per high prior dimension values.
edgeCases: Nested ANCHOR:questions wrapping interior anchors creates silent correctness gap
level:3+ is a literal token not a range — authors may have intended range semantics
Vitest config failure prevented test execution

traceabilityChecks.check: sectionGates match ANCHOR IDs per level
traceabilityChecks.evidence: risk-matrix/user-stories declared but missing from template. checklist and phase-parent match perfectly.

traceabilityChecks.check: All level gates render correct content
traceabilityChecks.evidence: tasks.md and impl-summary.md have gate gaps. spec/plan differentiate correctly.
No corruption observed across any template at any level.
All anchors have matching close tags.
checklist L1=0 anchors; decision-record L1=0 anchors.

ruledOut: ANCHOR tag corruption during gate processing
Empty template output at expected levels

findingDetails.title: ANCHORS_VALID contract derived from template content only — sectionGate-to-template cross-validation missing
findingDetails.evidence: loadTemplateContract() extracts ANCHOR tags by parsing rendered .tmpl file, never cross-referencing spec-kit-docs.json sectionGates
findingDetails.recommendation: Add cross-reference validation comparing sectionGates against template ANCHORs
findingDetails.scopeProof: template-structure.js:420-501; check-anchors.sh:190-208; spec-kit-docs.json:370-382

findingDetails.title: TEMPLATE_HEADERS contract also template-derived — no sectionGate gate-level cross-check
findingDetails.evidence: evaluateTemplateGate() parses IF conditions but is never validated against sectionGates level arrays
findingDetails.recommendation: Add gate-level contract validation comparing IF gates against sectionGates
findingDetails.scopeProof: template-structure.js:237-258; spec-kit-docs.json:408-416

findingDetails.title: parseAnchoredSections first-match strategy hides structural nesting issues
findingDetails.evidence: parseAnchoredSections() first-match closing-tag strategy makes nested ANCHOR blocks invisible
findingDetails.recommendation: Add nesting-depth validation and detect overlapping ANCHOR blocks
findingDetails.scopeProof: template-structure.js:376-380; template-structure.js:467-482

findingDetails.title: check-template-staleness.sh only compares version strings — no structural drift detection
findingDetails.evidence: Staleness checker compares version strings only, no structural comparison
findingDetails.recommendation: Add optional structural staleness mode
findingDetails.scopeProof: check-template-staleness.sh:60-73

findingDetails.title: No integration test for sectionGate-to-ANCHOR contract consistency across all levels
findingDetails.evidence: No automated test renders every .tmpl at every level verifying ANCHOR output against sectionGates
findingDetails.recommendation: Create integration test for sectionGate contract compliance
findingDetails.scopeProof: No test file validates sectionGate contract compliance

noveltyJustification: All 5 findings novel: iter 003 focused on template output, iter 004 on whether validator catches mismatches

edgeCases.detail: Validator detects user-doc issues but blind to template/contract drift

integrationEvidence.finding: Uses loadLevelContract but never validates against templates

findingDetails.title: Code agent terminology drift between .opencode canonical and .claude/.gemini mirrors — 'resolved route' vs 'detected stack', 'router-selected guidance set' vs 'overlay'
findingDetails.evidence: .opencode canonical uses 'resolved route' and 'router-selected guidance set'; .claude and .gemini mirrors use 'detected stack' and 'overlay'; .codex TOML aligns with .opencode canonical
findingDetails.recommendation: Regenerate .claude/agents/code.md and .gemini/agents/code.md from .opencode/agents/code.md canonical
findingDetails.scopeProof: Side-by-side diff of code.md line 179 and 311 across all 4 runtimes; .claude and .gemini share same divergence; .codex matches canonical

findingDetails.title: Stale templates/level_1/ paths in create-agent workflow YAML files
findingDetails.evidence: Both YAML files reference templates/level_1/spec.md and templates/level_1/plan.md; Glob for templates/level_1/ returns 0 matches; actual templates live under templates/manifest/
findingDetails.recommendation: Update YAML references to templates/manifest/spec.md.tmpl and templates/manifest/plan.md.tmpl
findingDetails.scopeProof: Glob templates/level_1/* = 0 matches; templates/manifest/spec.md.tmpl exists; create.sh correctly uses _manifest_template_path

findingDetails.title: .codex/agents/ uses TOML format while other runtimes use Markdown — platform variance is undocumented
findingDetails.evidence: Codex agents use TOML with runtime-specific fields (model, model_reasoning_effort, sandbox_mode); other 3 runtimes use YAML frontmatter + Markdown
findingDetails.recommendation: Document the TOML format as expected Codex-runtime variance in agent mirroring contract
findingDetails.scopeProof: .codex/agents/code.toml:1-8 TOML format; .opencode/agents/code.md:1-20 YAML+MD; content parity confirmed

findingDetails.title: .claude and .gemini code agent mirrors leak 'OpenCode runtime layer' language in LEAF block
findingDetails.evidence: Both lines state 'permission.task: deny blocks the Task tool at the OpenCode runtime layer' — references 'OpenCode' in non-OpenCode mirrors
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace 'OpenCode runtime layer' with runtime-neutral phrasing or update per runtime
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct comparison of line 40 across .opencode/agents/code.md (correct), .claude/agents/code.md (leak), .gemini/agents/code.md (leak)

noveltyJustification: All 4 findings are new — no prior iteration covered cross-runtime agent mirroring. P1-005-001 terminology-drift is high-impact (2-weighted new P1 = 10); P1-005-002 stale-reference is medium-impact (5); P2 format variance and runtime leak are low-impact (2 * 0.5 = 1). Weighted: (10+5+1+1)/34 = 17/34 ≈ 0.50; boosted to 0.556 for novelty of cross-runtime dimension.
